title: Remove a "storage mountopt" workaround
body:
PR #2115 (Kill Travis and Enable Bors, merged 2020-02-07) added
code that completely clobbered /etc/containers/storage.conf,
as a workaround for issue #1945. That closed 2020-04-29, and
the workaround is now a liability that has wasted developer
time. Remove it.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **tomsweeneyredhat** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @tomsweeneyredhat` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

```
 time="2020-12-17T15:52:23Z" level=error msg="The storage 'driver' option must be set in /etc/containers/storage.conf, guarantee proper operation."
```
well, that answers that.
We could go back to the sed line or discover why the /etc/containers/storage.conf in the VMs does not have a driver defined.
That has me curious (why the existing containers.conf is broken) but it's going to need

...going to need some sleuthwork. I checked the obvious place (automation_images), and couldn't find anything. I don't remember how to do the get-ci-vm thing. So let's try some debugging printfs.

/hold

Interesting: fedora 31/32 pass; only ubuntu-20 fails, and the [debug log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5154621809229824?command=setup#L151) confirms that:
```
# Default Storage Driver
driver = ""
```
Does anyone know the history behind this? Was there ever a bad containers-common that made it out, maybe only to ubuntu? Will a VM bump fix this? It seems suspicious that `driver = ""` is the exact symptom that @rhatdan was trying to work around in #2842. 

Anyhow, the good news is that fedora passes, so we might be able to proceed with this PR once we figure out the ubuntu problem.

The upstream containers/storage storage.conf has driver="", since upstream does not want to require a default.  In Fedora (As well as RHEL) we set the distro defaults including driver="overlay" and mountopt="nodev,metacopy=on".

I would bet the default in Ubuntu was just to grab the upstream default and that is causing the problems.
That would imply that all end users will see this warning, which would make it a packaging bug, no? Maybe something to track next year. Thanks for the context, that makes a lot of sense now.
Yes I think we need to get @lsm5 and friends to make sure this file has driver="overlay" defined in the storage.conf on Debian/Ubuntu platforms.

@edsantiago: PR needs rebase.

> Yes I think we need to get @lsm5 and friends to make sure this file has driver="overlay" defined in the storage.conf on Debian/Ubuntu platforms.

doing this now ...
